name: Cursa Vistabella Trail
name_original: El Penyagolosa — Trail run al "techo de la Comunidad Valenciana"
description: The Cursa Vistabella Trail is a difficult 6.0-mile jogging route through Penyagolosa Natural Park, featuring 1693 feet of elevation gain.
region_intro: Jogging routes around Parc Natural del Penyagolosa traverse a mountainous massif, often referred to as the "roof of the Valencian Community," with its highest peak reaching 1,814 meters. The region features varied terrain and elevations, from steep ascents to winding paths through valleys. Extensive woodlands, including Aleppo pine, Holm oak, and black pine forests, provide natural shade and fresh mountain air. These diverse landscapes offer a range of challenges for runners. Best jogging routes around Parc Natural del Penyagolosa The most popular jogging route is Cursa Vistabella Trail, a 6.0 miles (9.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 56 minutes to complete. This difficult route features a significant elevation gain, challenging runners with its gradients. Another top favourite among local runners is Bassa de l'Hort de l'Atzevar – Pinar de les Rambles loop from Ermitori de Sant Joan de Penyagolosa, a difficult 18.9 miles (30.5 km) path. This extensive loop explores varied terrain, including pine forests and valleys. Local runners also love the Penyagolosa Summit loop from Ermitori de Sant Joan de Penyagolosa, a 7.0 miles (11.3 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain towards the summit, often completed in about 1 hour 52 minutes. Jogging around Parc Natural del Penyagolosa is defined by its mountainous massif, diverse pine and oak forests, and panoramic views from higher elevations. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easier paths to more demanding routes. The routes in Parc Natural del Penyagolosa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from more than 3 reviews. More than 700 runners have used komoot to explore Parc Natural del Penyagolosa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Parc Natural del Penyagolosa?
A: Parc Natural del Penyagolosa offers a wide selection of running routes, with over 40 trails available. These routes cater to various fitness levels, ranging from easier paths to more demanding ascents.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running trails in Parc Natural del Penyagolosa?
A: The terrain in Parc Natural del Penyagolosa is highly varied, reflecting its mountainous massif. You can expect a mix of steep ascents, winding paths through valleys, and extensive woodlands. The trails traverse diverse ecosystems, including Aleppo pine, Holm oak, Valencian oak, and black pine forests, providing both challenging gradients and natural shade.

Q: Are there running routes suitable for beginners in Parc Natural del Penyagolosa?
A: While many routes in Parc Natural del Penyagolosa are challenging, there are options for less experienced runners. The park features at least one easy route, and several moderate trails that can be a good starting point before tackling the more difficult ascents. It's always recommended to check the route details for elevation and distance before you go.

Q: What are some challenging long-distance running routes in the park?
A: For those seeking a significant challenge, the park offers several demanding routes. One notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38175638" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bassa de l'Hort de l'Atzevar – Pinar de les Rambles loop from Ermitori de Sant Joan de Penyagolosa&lt;/a&gt;, an extensive 18.9-mile (30.5 km) path with substantial elevation gain. Another difficult route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24539028" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Xodos – Pla de la Creu loop from Parc Natural de Penyagolosa&lt;/a&gt;, covering over 21 km.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Parc Natural del Penyagolosa?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Parc Natural del Penyagolosa are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31871031" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Penyagolosa Summit loop from Ermitori de Sant Joan de Penyagolosa&lt;/a&gt; and the longer &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38175638" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bassa de l'Hort de l'Atzevar – Pinar de les Rambles loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural features or viewpoints can I see while jogging in Parc Natural del Penyagolosa?
A: Jogging in Parc Natural del Penyagolosa offers breathtaking natural scenery. From the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839679" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Penyagolosa Summit&lt;/a&gt;, you can enjoy spectacular panoramic views across the province of Castellón and into neighboring Aragón. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4154736" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranc de la Pegunta&lt;/a&gt; is another highlight, a lush black pine forest with a shaded ravine, offering a refreshing and scenic route.

Q: Are there any cultural landmarks or attractions along the running paths?
A: Absolutely. A significant cultural landmark is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5714710" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santuario de Sant Joan de Penyagolosa&lt;/a&gt;, an important pilgrimage destination often serving as a starting point for many trails, including routes to the summit. Its historical and cultural significance adds a unique dimension to your outdoor experience.

Q: Is parking available near the running trailheads in Parc Natural del Penyagolosa?
A: Yes, parking is generally available at key access points and trailheads within or near the park. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5618099" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Corralico&lt;/a&gt; serves as a trailhead to Penyagolosa Summit and is a common starting point, suggesting parking facilities nearby. It's advisable to check specific route descriptions for detailed parking information.

Q: Can I reach Parc Natural del Penyagolosa by public transport for a running trip?
A: While the park is somewhat remote, public transport options to nearby towns like Vistabella del Maestrat, Xodos, or Villahermosa del Río may exist. However, direct public transport to specific trailheads within the natural park can be limited. Planning your journey in advance and checking local bus schedules is recommended.

Q: What is the best season for trail running in Parc Natural del Penyagolosa?
A: The diverse climate of Parc Natural del Penyagolosa means different seasons offer unique experiences. Spring and autumn generally provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, ideal for running. Summer can be warm, but the extensive woodlands offer natural shade. Winter brings the possibility of snow and ice at higher elevations, which can add a challenging element for experienced trail runners.

Q: Are there any dog-friendly running routes in Parc Natural del Penyagolosa?
A: Parc Natural del Penyagolosa is generally welcoming to dogs, but specific regulations may apply to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of all visitors. It's always best to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or sensitive ecosystems, and to follow any posted signage regarding pet policies on individual trails.

Q: What do other runners enjoy the most about jogging in Parc Natural del Penyagolosa?
A: The komoot community highly rates the running experience in Parc Natural del Penyagolosa, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Runners frequently praise the varied terrain, the stunning panoramic views from higher elevations, and the refreshing atmosphere of the diverse pine and oak forests. The sense of natural beauty and the challenge of the mountainous trails are often highlighted.

Q: Are there any family-friendly running routes in Parc Natural del Penyagolosa?
A: While many routes in Parc Natural del Penyagolosa are quite challenging, there are easier paths that can be suitable for families looking to enjoy a lighter jog or walk together. These routes typically feature less elevation gain and shorter distances. It's recommended to consult route descriptions for difficulty and length to find the best fit for your family.
tips: The Penyagolosa (1,813 m) is popularly considered the geographical ceiling of the Valencian Community because all its slopes are within the province of Castellón, although it is officially Cerro Calderón or Alto de las Barracas (1,831 m), which has a slope in the Valencian territory of Rincón de Ademuz and another in the province of Teruel. The summit of Penyagolosa is a rocky trident with three spurs separated by vertical walls and vertiginous channels that fall towards the precipices of the south face. The views are magnificent, on clear days the Columbretes Islands appear on the Mediterranean horizon.

faqs.question: Is the Cursa Vistabella Trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The Cursa Vistabella Trail, as mapped on komoot, is rated as 'difficult' due to its significant elevation gain over a relatively short distance. While the main race course is a demanding 28 km mountain race, this specific 9.7 km route still presents a challenging experience with over 500 meters of ascent. It's best suited for experienced trail runners or hikers with good physical conditioning.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Cursa Vistabella Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail predominantly features natural, non-asphalted paths, making up about 90% of the route. You'll encounter a mix of faster sections and technical ascents, particularly in the latter half. Some parts are exposed to the sun, while others offer welcome shade. Expect varied mountain terrain typical of the Penyagolosa Natural Park.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Cursa Vistabella Trail?
faqs.answer: Specific parking details for this exact komoot route are not provided, but Vistabella del Maestrat, the nearest town, is the usual starting point for trails in the area. You would typically find parking within or on the outskirts of the town, especially if you are participating in the organized race. It's advisable to check local signage or the official race website for event-specific parking arrangements if you plan to visit during the Cursa Vistabella race.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to visit the Cursa Vistabella Trail?
faqs.answer: The Cursa Vistabella race often takes place in August, which can be very hot with significant sun exposure. For a more comfortable experience, consider visiting in spring (April-June) or early autumn (September-October) when temperatures are milder. These seasons offer pleasant conditions for tackling the trail's challenges and enjoying the scenery.

faqs.question: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The entire route winds through the stunning Penyagolosa Natural Park, offering a unique natural environment. A significant highlight along the way is the Penyagolosa Summit, which provides panoramic views and is a celebrated point for trail enthusiasts.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other trails. Notably, it covers significant sections of the Cursa Vistabella (59%) and Llucena - Sant Joan de Penyagolosa (40%). You'll also find it crossing parts of the Sender de la Lluna Plena (17%) and El Camí IT6TR1 El Camí Central: de Castelló de la Plana a Montserrat (17%).

faqs.question: Is the Cursa Vistabella Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Penyagolosa Natural Park generally allows dogs, it's crucial to keep them on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Always practice Leave No Trace principles, including packing out all pet waste. Check for any specific local regulations or temporary restrictions, especially during organized events like the Cursa Vistabella race.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for the Penyagolosa Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there is no entrance fee to access the Penyagolosa Natural Park for hiking or trail running. However, if you plan to participate in the Cursa Vistabella race, you will need to register and pay a race entry fee. For specific activities or larger groups, it's always a good idea to check the official park website for any updated regulations or permit requirements.

faqs.question: What are the regulations for visiting Penyagolosa Natural Park?
faqs.answer: When visiting Penyagolosa Natural Park, remember to stay on marked trails, respect wildlife, and carry out all your trash. Open fires are strictly prohibited, and camping may require specific permits. Dogs must be kept on a leash. Always be mindful of the natural environment and local regulations to preserve this 'landscape and emotional jewel'.

faqs.question: Is the komoot route for the Cursa Vistabella Trail a loop?
faqs.answer: Yes, the 9.7 km komoot route for the Cursa Vistabella Trail is designed as a loop, starting and ending at approximately the same point. This allows you to experience the varied terrain and scenery without needing to arrange transportation back to a different starting location.

faqs.question: What is the difference between the komoot route and the official Cursa Vistabella race?
faqs.answer: The komoot route provided is a 9.7 km 'jogging' trail with 516m of elevation gain, rated 'difficult'. The official Cursa Vistabella race, however, is a much more demanding mountain race, typically covering 28 km with around 1500 meters of positive elevation gain. While the komoot route shares the name and is in the same area, it represents a shorter, albeit still challenging, experience compared to the full race.
